title: Check LED polarity with a multimeter
description: Use the diode mode of a digital multimeter to confirm an LED's orientation before wiring. Wear safety goggles for protection.

dialogue.text:
Grab your digital multimeter, a loose 5 mm LED, and safety goggles.
We'll use diode test mode to identify the positive lead.

With your goggles on, set the meter to diode mode. Touch the red probe to the LED's long leg and the black to the short leg without forcing the leads.
The LED should glow dimly or show about 1.8 V. Reverse the probes and it should stay dark or show OL.

Nice! Now you know which lead is positive.
Disconnect the probes and store the LED for your next circuit.
